ddls:
CREATE TABLE "performance_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "median" REAL /* Median value of the performance metric */,
  "lower_90" REAL /* Lower 90th percentile value */,
  "lower_50" REAL /* Lower 50th percentile value */,
  "upper_50" REAL /* Upper 50th percentile value */,
  "upper_90" REAL /* Upper 90th percentile value */,
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the region related to the metric */,
  "source" TEXT /* Source of the data (e.g., cases, admissions) */,
  "model" TEXT /* Model used for calculation (e.g., Single breakpoint) */,
  "metric_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was recorded */,
  "facility_id" INTEGER /* ID of the healthcare facility where the metric was recorded */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient (optional, for more granular data) */,
  "metric_category" TEXT /* Category of the metric (e.g., clinical, administrative, financial) */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Confidence level of the metric (e.g., 95%) */,
  "unit_of_measurement" TEXT /* Unit in which the metric is measured (e.g., days, percentage) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_source FOREIGN KEY ("source") REFERENCES data_sources ("source_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_region_id FOREIGN KEY ("region_id") REFERENCES regions ("region_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_metrics_model FOREIGN KEY ("model") REFERENCES models ("model_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "regions" (
  "region_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each region */,
  "region_name" TEXT /* Name of the region (e.g., South West, Wales) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the region's characteristics */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the region */,
  "geographic_area" TEXT /* Geographic area (e.g., urban, rural) */,
  "healthcare_coverage" TEXT /* Level of healthcare coverage in the region (e.g., high, medium, low) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("region_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., cases, admissions) */,
  "source_type" TEXT /* Type of the data source (e.g., clinical, administrative) */,
  "source_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the data source */,
  "data_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which data is collected (e.g., daily, weekly, monthly) */,
  "data_quality_score" INTEGER /* Score indicating the quality of the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "models" (
  "model_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each model */,
  "model_name" TEXT /* Name of the model used for calculations */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the model's application */,
  "model_accuracy" REAL /* Accuracy of the model */,
  "model_complexity" INTEGER /* Complexity of the model (e.g., 1 for simple, 5 for complex) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("model_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for logging in to the system */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, analyst, practitioner) */,
  "full_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date of the user's last login */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the user account (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the metric */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the metric was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "device_type" TEXT /* Type of device used (e.g., desktop, mobile) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_metric_id FOREIGN KEY ("metric_id") REFERENCES performance_metrics ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_templates" (
  "template_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report template */,
  "template_name" TEXT /* Name of the template */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the report template */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the template */,
  "template_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., summary, detailed) */,
  "last_modified_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last modified the template */,
  "last_modified_date" TEXT /* Date when the template was last modified */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("template_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_templates_last_modified_by FOREIGN KEY ("last_modified_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_templates_created_by FOREIGN KEY ("created_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "template_id" INTEGER /* ID of the template used to generate the report */,
  "generated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who generated the report */,
  "generation_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was generated */,
  "report_data" TEXT /* Content of the report */,
  "report_status" TEXT /* Status of the report (e.g., draft, finalized) */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, Excel) */,
  "report_length" INTEGER /* Length of the report in pages or words */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_generated_by FOREIGN KEY ("generated_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_template_id FOREIGN KEY ("template_id") REFERENCES report_templates ("template_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "feedback" (
  "feedback_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each feedback entry */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report being reviewed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user providing feedback */,
  "feedback_date" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was submitted */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Comments from the user */,
  "feedback_rating" INTEGER /* Rating (1-5) given by the user */,
  "is_resolved" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the feedback has been resolved */,
  "resolved_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who resolved the feedback */,
  "resolved_date" TEXT /* Date when the feedback was resolved */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("feedback_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_resolved_by FOREIGN KEY ("resolved_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_feedback_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "notification_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "notification_type" TEXT /* Type of notification (e.g., report, update) */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the notification has been read */,
  "read_date" TEXT /* Date when the notification was read */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., admin, analyst, practitioner) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role's responsibilities */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSON string containing the permissions associated with the role */,
  "last_updated_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last updated the role */,
  "last_updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the role was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role mapping */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role associated with the user */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Date when the user was assigned the role */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* Date when the role assignment ends (null if active) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
